title: Match-rate drop across all networks
scope: Customer: Motive Care (propagated to all network-level match rates)
detected_by: robust_zscore on network match_rate; decomposition by customer isolated Motive Care
root_cause: Motive Care onboarded 2025-11 with address lines concatenated into a single field by their EHR export, degrading demographic matching on every downstream network (~25 pt match-rate drop for their panel). Network-level series dropped in lockstep — a correlated cross-network drop indicates a demographics/customer-mix cause, not a network outage.
customer_impact: Motive Care patients returned sparse histories; national match KPI dropped ~2.5 pts.
resolution: Address parser fix deployed in their intake pipeline 2026-03; match rate recovered to expected range within one cycle.

title: Carequality coverage collapse in South-Atlantic states
scope: States: MD VA NC SC GA FL / Network: Carequality
root_cause: A major South-Atlantic Carequality implementer had a responder outage during a data-center migration; queries timed out and returned empty bundles. Confirmed by records_returned collapsing ~70% while patients_queried was flat.
customer_impact: One-month gap in EHR-sourced conditions/encounters/documents for ~6 states; customers with South-Atlantic panels (Oak Grove, Bluebird) saw the largest dips.
resolution: Implementer restored responders 2026-03-04; backfill queries recovered historical documents. Added per-implementer responder health monitoring.

title: Quest lab freshness SLO breach
scope: Network: Quest Labs / Data type: labs (all states)
root_cause: Quest changed their result-feed schema (new OBX segments) in early April; our ingest quarantined non-conforming messages for manual review, delaying availability. Volume was unaffected — only latency — which pointed at ingest, not the feed itself.
customer_impact: Lab-dependent workflows (e.g. Cascade Kidney eGFR monitoring) saw results arriving ~2.5 weeks late; SLO (7d) breached 2 months.
resolution: Parser updated 2026-05-28; quarantine drained. Added schema-drift canary on inbound lab feeds.

title: ADT coverage loss in WA/OR via PointClickCare
scope: States: WA OR / Network: PointClickCare / Data type: adt_events
detected_by: level_shift detector (6-mo trailing vs leading mean; z-score missed it)
root_cause: A regional contract lapse removed Pacific-Northwest facilities from our PointClickCare feed. Because the loss persisted 6 months it shifted the series median and evaded z-score detection — caught by the level-shift detector. Bamboo Health partially masked the gap in blended ADT coverage.
customer_impact: Harborview VBC and Cascade Kidney (WA/OR-concentrated) lost ~95% of PCC-sourced admission alerts; readmission-outreach programs degraded.
resolution: Contract renewed 2026-06; feed restored. Blended-network dashboards now also break out per-partner coverage so one partner can't mask another.

title: Structural document coverage gap in TX/OK/MS (+FL/AR)
scope: States: TX OK MS FL AR / Data type: documents / Networks: Carequality CommonWell
detected_by: cross-sectional gap detection (>40% below national mean for 3+ months)
root_cause: Not an incident — a structural gap. Rural provider participation in nationwide EHR networks is low in these states, so document retrieval underperforms the national mean by 40-45% persistently. TEFCA QHIN onboarding is expected to close part of the gap.
customer_impact: Customers with Gulf-state panels (Oak Grove, Bluebird) consistently receive thinner document histories; set expectations during onboarding and in coverage reports.
resolution: Mitigation: prioritize regional HIE partnerships in TX/OK/MS; track TEFCA ramp quarterly. Not resolvable by engineering alone.
